response: during the cold war the united states and the soviet union were the two main powers that dominated the world. the us was led by president john f kennedy and his administration, while the soviet union was led by josef stalins successor, nikita khrushchev. the cold war was a period of tension and rivalry between these two superpowers, with both sides engaging in military build-up and ideological conflict. the us was also supported by various allies, including canada, australia, and japan, while the soviet union had allies in the middle east and africa. the cold war ended in 1991 with the collapse of the soviet union and the rise of new nations in europe and asia. the us was the dominant power in the world after the cold war, with a strong military and economic influence. the cold war was a time of great uncertainty and fear, with both superpowers trying to prevent the spread of communism and the rise of nuclear weapons. the us and the soviet union were in charge of the
